seoTitle: Migrate From QuickBooks to Zoho Books Without Data Loss
metaDescription: Move your accounting data from QuickBooks Online to Zoho Books cleanly, from exporting CSVs to mapping your Chart of Accounts.
intro: Switching accounting platforms is risky if you lose historical data along the way. This guide shows how to export your QuickBooks Online data and import it into Zoho Books while keeping your Chart of Accounts, customers, and balances intact.

steps.heading: Export from QuickBooks
steps.body: Generate and download your customer, vendor, products/services, and Chart of Accounts lists as CSV or Excel files.

steps.heading: Set up Zoho Books
steps.body: Open Zoho Books, go to Settings, then Organization Profile, and set your base currency, fiscal year, and tax system to match your QuickBooks setup.

steps.heading: Start the data import
steps.body: Go to the Sales or Purchases tab in Zoho Books and open the Import Data utility.

steps.heading: Map your fields
steps.body: Drag your exported CSV into the upload zone and align QuickBooks header fields, like Company Name and Email, to the matching Zoho Books fields.

steps.heading: Enter opening balances and verify
steps.body: Go to Settings, then Opening Balances, enter your closing ledger metrics, and pull a Profit & Loss report in Zoho Books to confirm it aligns with your legacy QuickBooks reports.

faq.question: Will I lose historical data by switching to Zoho Books?
faq.answer: Not if you export your customer, vendor, and account data from QuickBooks first and properly import it with matched field mapping and opening balances.

faq.question: Do I need to manually recreate my Chart of Accounts?
faq.answer: You export it as a spreadsheet from QuickBooks and map the categories into Zoho Books during the import process.

faq.question: How do I verify the migration was successful?
faq.answer: Run matching Trial Balance or Profit & Loss reports in both platforms and compare the totals.

faq.question: What settings should I check before importing data?
faq.answer: Confirm your base currency, fiscal year lock dates, and tax system in Zoho Books match your QuickBooks preferences first.
